functions.name: CNContactPicker.Close
functions.description: Closes the picker.

functions.name: CNContactPicker.Current
functions.description: Queries the current contact. Can be called in trigger script to query current contact selected. For multiple selection mode, this returns list of contacts.

functions.name: CNContactPicker.SetTrigger
functions.description: Sets the script trigger. This script is called when a contact is selected.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the FileMaker File that contains the script.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to run.

examples.label: Setup trigger:
examples.text_code:
# use picker sheet/popover to select contact
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "CNContactPicker.SetTrigger"; Get(FileName); "Picked") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
CNContactPicker.Show
") ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to show picker" ;
$r
]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> use picker sheet/popover to select contact</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CNContactPicker.SetTrigger"; Get(FileName); "Picked") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CNContactPicker.Show") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to show picker" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CNContactPicker.Show
functions.description: Shows the contact picker to select a contact. Use CNContactPicker.Current later to get the result.

parameters.name: Multi or Window
parameters.description: For iOS: Whether to allow multiple selection. Pass 1 for multiple or 0 for single selection. Default is single selection. For MacOS: the window reference name/number.

parameters.name: Position
parameters.description: Where to position the popover. Can be "left" for left of the FileMaker window. Can be "right" for right side. Can be "mouse" to place where mouse is, e.g. when using with button. Can be "toolbar" to center on window hanging from toolbar.

examples.label: Show picker:
examples.text_code:
# use picker sheet/popover to select contact
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
CNContactPicker.SetTrigger
"; Get(FileName); "Picked") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "CNContactPicker.Show") ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to show picker" ;
$r
]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> use picker sheet/popover to select contact</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CNContactPicker.SetTrigger"; Get(FileName); "Picked") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CNContactPicker.Show") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to show picker" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Show at mouse positon in front window:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "CNContactPicker.Show"; 0; "mouse")
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CNContactPicker.Show"; 0; "mouse")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ECKey.Description

parameters.name: ECKey
parameters.description: The reference number for the key.

functions.name: ECKey.Duplicate
functions.description: Creates a copy of the key. Please use ECKey.Release later to free memory.

parameters.name: ECKey
parameters.description: The reference number for the key.

functions.name: ECKey.ES256Sign
functions.description: Signs the given data with ES256. Can be used to build a JWT with ES256.

parameters.name: ECKey
parameters.description: The reference number for the key.

parameters.name: Data
parameters.description: The hex encoded data.

parameters.name: DataEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for data parameter. Default is UTF-8. Pass base64 if the data is base64 encoded or pass hex if it is hex encoded.

parameters.name: OutputEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for the result. e.g. Hex Default is Base64.

examples.label: Sign and verify:
examples.text_code:
Let([
id =
MBS
( "
ECKey.CurveNameToID
"; "P-256" );
ECKey =
MBS
( "
ECKey.Generate
"; id );
signature =
MBS
( "ECKey.ES256Sign"; ECKey; "Hello World"; "UTF-8"; "Base64" );
verify =

("
ECKey.ES256Verify

description =

("
ECKey.Description
"; ECKey);

("
ECKey.Release
"; ECKey)
]; signature & ¶ & verify & ¶ & description)

examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
  id = MBS( "ECKey.CurveNameToID"; "P-256" );
  ECKey = MBS( "ECKey.Generate"; id );
  signature = MBS( "ECKey.ES256Sign"; ECKey; "Hello World"; "UTF-8"; "Base64" );
  verify = MBS("ECKey.ES256Verify"; ECKey; "Hello World"; "UTF-8"; signature; "Base64");
  description = MBS("ECKey.Description"; ECKey);
  r = MBS("ECKey.Release"; ECKey)
]; signature & ¶ & verify & ¶ & description)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ECKey.ES256Verify
functions.signature: MBS( "ECKey.ES256Verify"; ECKey; Data; DataEncoding; SignatureData { ; SignatureDataEncoding } )
functions.description: Verifies the digital signature with ES256. We perform a hash with SHA256 of the data and verify the result.

parameters.name: ECKey
parameters.description: The reference number for the key.

parameters.name: Data
parameters.description: The data.

parameters.name: DataEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for data parameter. Default is UTF-8. Pass base64 if the data is base64 encoded or pass hex if it is hex encoded.

parameters.name: SignatureData
parameters.description: The hex encoded signature.

parameters.name: SignatureDataEncoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for signature parameter. Default is Base64.

functions.name: ECKey.Generate
functions.description: Generates a new pair of private and public keys. You can use ECKey.ReadPrivateKey and ECKey.ReadPublicKey functions to get the keys. Please use ECKey.Release later to free memory.

parameters.name: CurveID
parameters.description: The curve ID to use.

examples.label: Generate keys and store in fields:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$key
; Value:
MBS
("ECKey.Generate"; 711)]
Set Field [
ECKey Test::KeyInfo
;
MBS
("
ECKey.Description
";
$key
)]
Set Field [
ECKey Test::PrivateKey
;
MBS
("
ECKey.PrivateKey
";
$key
)]
Set Field [
ECKey Test::PublicKey
;
MBS
("
ECKey.PublicKey
";
$key
)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
ECKey.Release
";
$key
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ECKey.Generate"; 711)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$key</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("ECKey.Description"; $key)</Calculation>
    <Field table="ECKey Test" id="99999" name="KeyInfo"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("ECKey.PrivateKey"; $key)</Calculation>
    <Field table="ECKey Test" id="99999" name="PrivateKey"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("ECKey.PublicKey"; $key)</Calculation>
    <Field table="ECKey Test" id="99999" name="PublicKey"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ECKey.Release"; $key)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

parameters.name: Data

parameters.name: ECKey
parameters.description: The reference number for the key.

parameters.name: ECKey
parameters.description: The reference number for the key.

functions.name: ECKey.Verify
functions.description: Verifies the digital signature. Returns OK on success. Returns error if data, signature and public key don't belong together. We highly recommend to use a hashed value for the data parameter, e.g. use Hash.SHA512 function to get a 512bit hash for your data.

parameters.name: ECKey
parameters.description: The reference number for the key.

parameters.name: Data
parameters.description: The hex encoded data.

parameters.name: SignatureData
parameters.description: The hex encoded signature.

functions.name: EventMonitor.Clear
functions.description: Clears last event captured. You should not need to call this, except maybe on the end of the script to make sure you clear the last event for next script call. This includes which button was last clicked. Plugin version 9.4 also forgets whether it was a double click.

functions.name: EventMonitor.ClearCardWindow
functions.description: Clears trigger for card window. Please call in close trigger for the card layout and in script triggered by EventMonitor.SetCardWindow .

examples.label: Close script:
examples.text_code:
Close Window [ Name: "Card" ; Current file ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("EventMonitor.ClearCardWindow") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="121" name="Close Window">
    <LimitToWindowsOfCurrentFile state="True"/>
    <Window value="ByName"/>
    <Name>
      <Calculation>"Card"</Calculation>
    </Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("EventMonitor.ClearCardWindow") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: EventMonitor.GetEnabled
functions.description: Queries whether event monitor is enabled. Returns 1 if enabled and 0 if disabled.

functions.name: EventMonitor.Install
functions.description: Installs event monitor. This allows the plugin to keep track of mouse events, so the EventMonitor.LastMouseClickButton function can know what mouse button was pressed.

functions.name: EventMonitor.LastMouseClickButton
functions.description: Queries the index of the last pressed mouse button. Returns 1 for left, 2 for right and 3 for other. (other is Mac only currently) Please call EventMonitor.Install earlier in your scripts once on startup of solution. Call this function in a script triggered by a mouse click (e.g. on a button) to see if mouse event triggering the script was using right or left mouse button. See also RemoteControl.MouseButtons function for live status of mouse. With v12.5 this function may also return 4 and 5 for mouse buttons.

functions.name: EventMonitor.LastMouseClickIsDoubleClick
functions.description: Queries if last recognized mouse click is a double click. Returns 0 for single click and 1 for double click. Please call EventMonitor.Install earlier in your scripts once on startup of solution. Call this function in a script triggered by a mouse click (e.g. on a button) to see if mouse event triggering the script was a double click.

functions.name: EventMonitor.LastMousePositionX
functions.description: Queries last mouse position. Relative to top/left of main screen. Please call EventMonitor.Install earlier in your scripts once on startup of solution. See also RemoteControl.MouseX .

parameters.name: PerWindow
parameters.description: Pass 1 to get coordinates relative to the window at mouse position (Mac) or front window (Win). Or pass 0 to get screen coordinates. (default)

functions.name: EventMonitor.LastMousePositionY
functions.description: Queries last mouse position. Relative to top/left of main screen. Please call EventMonitor.Install earlier in your scripts once on startup of solution. See also RemoteControl.MouseY .

parameters.name: PerWindow
parameters.description: Pass 1 to get coordinates relative to the window at mouse position (Mac) or front window (Win). Or pass 0 to get screen coordinates. (default)

functions.name: EventMonitor.SetCardWindow
functions.signature: MBS( "EventMonitor.SetCardWindow"; WindowRef; X; Y; Width; Height; FileName; ScriptName { ; ScriptParameter; ScriptControl } )
functions.description: Sets script trigger for clicks outside card. You can show a card and have the MBS Plugin watch for mouse clicks outside the card area. The plugin can trigger a script. As script triggers are queued up, please make sure you handle various cases like several clicks queue up. Please call EventMonitor.Install once before and make sure it’s enabled. Since we lookup the window size for the card, please don't name your window and the card window the same. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: X
parameters.description: X position of the card in the parent window. Can be negative for automatic center position.

parameters.name: Y
parameters.description: Y position of the card in the parent window. Can be negative for automatic center position. You may need to include space for formating, status toolbar and menu bar (inside window on Windows).

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: The width of the card window.

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: The height of the card window.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name where to find the script.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to trigger when a click is received outside the card area.

parameters.name: ScriptParameter
parameters.description: The text parameter to pass to the script.

parameters.name: ScriptControl
parameters.description: The script control mode. Can be Pause, Halt, Exit or Resume. Default is Pause. May be ignored by FileMaker.

examples.label: Install trigger:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
EventMonitor.Install
" ) ]
Set Variable [
$$cardShowing
; Value: 1 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "EventMonitor.SetCardWindow"; Get(WindowName); -1; -1; 600; 400; Get(FileName); "Close Script" ) ]
New Window [ Style: Card ; Name: "Card" ; Using layout: “Tabelle” ; Height: 400 ; Width: 600 ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "EventMonitor.Install" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$$cardShowing</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

      <Calculation>MBS( "EventMonitor.SetCardWindow"; Get(WindowName); -1; -1; 600; 400; Get(FileName); "Close Script" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="122" name="New Window">
    <LayoutDestination value="SelectedLayout"/>
    <Name>
      <Calculation>"Card"</Calculation>
    </Name>
    <Height>
      <Calculation>400</Calculation>
    </Height>
    <Width>
      <Calculation>600</Calculation>
    </Width>
    <NewWndStyles DimParentWindow="Yes" Toolbars="No" MenuBar="No" Style="Card" Close="Yes" Minimize="No" Maximize="No" Resize="No" Styles="3222339600"/>
    <Layout id="10" name="Tabelle"/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Font.Activate

parameters.name: Local

functions.result: Returns "OK" or error number from OS.

functions.name: Font.CalculateTextHeight
functions.signature: MBS( "Font.CalculateTextHeight"; Text { ; Warp Width; Font; Size } )
functions.description: Calculates height of text. The plugin uses system font rendering to calculate height given your wrap width. This may be different from what FileMaker renders with it's own engine. You can use the measured size to detect if text fits a field. Mac result in logical points, but on Windows in pixels.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to measure.

parameters.name: Warp Width
parameters.description: The width of the text block to use for wrapping. If < 50 will be set to 50 to avoid endless loops.

parameters.name: Font
parameters.description: The font name. Using System font in case of empty parameter.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: The font size to use. Default is system font size.

examples.label: Calculates text height for a given text:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "Font.CalculateTextHeight"; "Hello World. Just a test."; 100; "Arial"; 12 )
Example result:
36
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Font.CalculateTextHeight"; "Hello World. Just a test."; 100; "Arial"; 12 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Font.CalculateTextWidth

You can use the measured size to detect if text fits a field. Mac result in logical points, but on Windows in pixels. See also GMImage.FontTypeMetrics .

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to measure.

parameters.name: Font
parameters.description: The font name. Using System font in case of empty parameter.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: The font size to use. Default is system font size.

functions.name: GetSystemUIModeOptions
functions.description: Queries the current system UI mode options. See the SetSystemUIMode function for details on the possible values. This function checks for a license. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 18th August 2014 GetSystemUIMode - HadErrors 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: HadErrors
functions.description: Queries error counter. Call this on the end of a script to query how many errors happened. You need to call ClearErrors function before on the start of the script to reset counter. Use ErrorLog function to read the log. The counter increases whenever a plugin function returns an error. See also IsError function. Errors are tracked per thread, so on a server each PSoS session has its own error log. And it includes errors from subscripts.

examples.label: Log errors from FileMaker and MBS to a table:
examples.text_code:
# LogErrors in file HotKeys
# check for FileMaker errors
Set Variable [
$path
; Value:
MBS
("
Path.AddPathComponent
";
MBS
("
Folders.UserDocuments
"); "ScriptErrors.log") ]
If [
MBS
("
Files.FileExists
";
$path
) ]
# save the content of FileMaker's log file to the database table
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
FM.InsertRecord
"; Get(FileName); "Errors"; "Details";
MBS
("
Text.ReadTextFile
";
$path
; "UTF-8")) ]
# make sure FileMaker makes a new file next time
Set Error Logging [ Off ]
# delete the file
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Files.Delete
";
$path
) ]
End If
# check for
MBS
errors on the way
If [
MBS
("HadErrors") > 0 ]
Set Variable [
$Log
; Value:
MBS
( "ErrorLog") ]
# and write the JSON to a table
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
FM.InsertRecord
"; Get(FileName); "Errors"; "Details";
$Log
) ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> LogErrors in file HotKeys</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> check for FileMaker errors</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Path.AddPathComponent"; MBS("Folders.UserDocuments"); "ScriptErrors.log") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("Files.FileExists"; $path) </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> save the content of FileMaker's log file to the database table</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("FM.InsertRecord"; Get(FileName); "Errors"; "Details"; MBS("Text.ReadTextFile"; $path; "UTF-8")) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> make sure FileMaker makes a new file next time</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="200" name="Set Error Logging">
    <Option state="False"/>
    <DisableStepCollapsed state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> delete the file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Files.Delete"; $path) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> check for MBS errors on the way</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("HadErrors") > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ErrorLog") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Log</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and write the JSON to a table</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("FM.InsertRecord"; Get(FileName); "Errors"; "Details"; $Log) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: HealthKit.FitzpatrickSkinType
functions.description: Queries skin type. Returns an object encapsulating the user's Fitzpatrick skin type. Before calling this method, the application should request authorization to access objects with the FitzpatrickSkinType type. This represents the skin type of an individual based on the Fitzpatrick scale. The Fitzpatrick scale is a numerical classification for skin color based on the skin's response to sun exposure in terms of the degree of burning and tanning. I      Pale white skin that always burns easily in the sun and never tans. II     White skin that burns easily and tans minimally. III    White to light brown skin that burns moderately and tans uniformly. IV     Beige-olive, lightly tanned skin that burns minimally and tans moderately. V      Brown skin that rarely burns and tans profusely. VI     Dark brown to black skin that never burns and tans profusely.

examples.label: Show skin type:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Skin Type" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>MBS( "HealthKit.FitzpatrickSkinType" ) </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: HealthKit.RequestAuthorizationToShareTypes
functions.description: Requests authorization from the user. Prompts the user to authorize the application for reading and saving objects of the given types. Before attempting to execute queries or save objects, the application should first request authorization from the user to read and share every type of object for which the application may require access. The request is performed asynchronously and its completion will be executed on an arbitrary background queue after the user has responded.  If the user has already chosen whether to grant the application access to all of the types provided, then the completion will be called without prompting the user. We call optionally your script to pass in the result. You get a list of values. First entry is Success or Cancel. Second value is the error message. The success parameter of the completion indicates whether prompting the user, if necessary, completed successfully and was not cancelled by the user.  It does NOT indicate whether the application was granted authorization. To customize the messages displayed on the authorization sheet, set the following keys in your app's  Info.plist file. Set the NSHealthShareUsageDescription key to customize the message for reading data. Set the NSHealthUpdateUsageDescription key to customize the message for writing data. Supported quantity types: Body Measurements: BodyFatPercentage, BodyMass, BodyMassIndex, Height, LeanBodyMass, WaistCircumference. Fitness: StepCount, DistanceWalkingRunning, DistanceCycling, DistanceWheelchair, BasalEnergyBurned, ActiveEnergyBurned, FlightsClimbed, NikeFuel, AppleExerciseTime, PushCount, DistanceSwimming, SwimmingStrokeCount, VO2Max. Vitals: HeartRate, BodyTemperature, BasalBodyTemperature, BloodPressureSystolic, BloodPressureDiastolic, RespiratoryRate. Results: OxygenSaturation, PeripheralPerfusionIndex, BloodGlucose, NumberOfTimesFallen, ElectrodermalActivity, InhalerUsage, InsulinDelivery, BloodAlcoholContent, ForcedVitalCapacity, ForcedExpiratoryVolume1, PeakExpiratoryFlowRate. Nutrition: DietaryFatTotal, DietaryFatPolyunsaturated, DietaryFatMonounsaturated, DietaryFatSaturated, DietaryCholesterol, DietarySodium, DietaryCarbohydrates, DietaryFiber, DietarySugar, DietaryEnergyConsumed, DietaryProtein, DietaryVitaminA, DietaryVitaminB6, DietaryVitaminB12, DietaryVitaminC, DietaryVitaminD, DietaryVitaminE, DietaryVitaminK, DietaryCalcium, DietaryIron, DietaryThiamin, DietaryRiboflavin, DietaryNiacin, DietaryFolate, DietaryBiotin, DietaryPantothenicAcid, DietaryPhosphorus, DietaryIodine, DietaryMagnesium, DietaryZinc, DietarySelenium, DietaryCopper, DietaryManganese, DietaryChromium, DietaryMolybdenum, DietaryChloride, DietaryPotassium, DietaryCaffeine, DietaryWater. UVExposure Sample Types include Quantity types plus: Workout SleepAnalysis, AppleStandHour, CervicalMucusQuality, OvulationTestResult, MenstrualFlow, IntermenstrualBleeding, SexualActivity and MindfulSession. BloodPressure and CDA. Object Types include all above plus: activitySummary BiologicalSex BloodType DateOfBirth FitzpatrickSkinType WheelchairUse Please enable HealthKit for the app entitlements for Xcode, add HealthKit framework and set NSHealthShareUsageDescription descriptor for info.plist.

parameters.name: ReadList

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name of the script to call.

parameters.name: ScriptName

≠ "OK" ]

$r
]

functions.name: HealthKit.SampleQuery
functions.signature: MBS( "HealthKit.SampleQuery"; QuantityType; Unit; StartDate; EndDate { ; SortKey; ascending; Limit } )
functions.description: Queries sample data from the database. Before calling this method, the application should request authorization to access objects with the desired quantity type. Supported quantity types: Body Measurements: BodyFatPercentage, BodyMass, BodyMassIndex, Height, LeanBodyMass, WaistCircumference. Fitness: StepCount, DistanceWalkingRunning, DistanceCycling, DistanceWheelchair, BasalEnergyBurned, ActiveEnergyBurned, FlightsClimbed, NikeFuel, AppleExerciseTime, PushCount, DistanceSwimming, SwimmingStrokeCount, VO2Max. Vitals: HeartRate, BodyTemperature, BasalBodyTemperature, BloodPressureSystolic, BloodPressureDiastolic, RespiratoryRate. Results: OxygenSaturation, PeripheralPerfusionIndex, BloodGlucose, NumberOfTimesFallen, ElectrodermalActivity, InhalerUsage, InsulinDelivery, BloodAlcoholContent, ForcedVitalCapacity, ForcedExpiratoryVolume1, PeakExpiratoryFlowRate. Nutrition: DietaryFatTotal, DietaryFatPolyunsaturated, DietaryFatMonounsaturated, DietaryFatSaturated, DietaryCholesterol, DietarySodium, DietaryCarbohydrates, DietaryFiber, DietarySugar, DietaryEnergyConsumed, DietaryProtein, DietaryVitaminA, DietaryVitaminB6, DietaryVitaminB12, DietaryVitaminC, DietaryVitaminD, DietaryVitaminE, DietaryVitaminK, DietaryCalcium, DietaryIron, DietaryThiamin, DietaryRiboflavin, DietaryNiacin, DietaryFolate, DietaryBiotin, DietaryPantothenicAcid, DietaryPhosphorus, DietaryIodine, DietaryMagnesium, DietaryZinc, DietarySelenium, DietaryCopper, DietaryManganese, DietaryChromium, DietaryMolybdenum, DietaryChloride, DietaryPotassium, DietaryCaffeine, DietaryWater. UVExposure

parameters.name: QuantityType
parameters.description: The quantity type to lookup.

parameters.name: Unit
parameters.description: The unit to use. Can be SI unit or for example count for counters. See Apple documentation for details.

parameters.name: StartDate
parameters.description: The start date for the search.

parameters.name: EndDate
parameters.description: The end date for the search.

parameters.name: SortKey
parameters.description: How to sort. Can be StartDate or EndDate.

parameters.name: ascending
parameters.description: Whether to prefer ascending instead of descending. Pass 1 for ascending or 0 for descending.

parameters.name: Limit
parameters.description: The limit for search. Default 0 for no limit.

functions.result: Returns values as JSON or error.

functions.name: HealthKit.isHealthDataAvailable
functions.description: Checks whether health data is available. Returns 1 if HealthKit is supported on the device. HealthKit is not supported on all iOS devices. Call isHealthDataAvailable before attempting to use other parts of the framework. Please ask for a plugin with HealthKit included. We often ship without this feature as you can only use it in apps which opt-in for HealthKit.

examples.label: Check status:
examples.text_code:
Show Custom Dialog [ "Health available" ; Let ( [ r = Let ( [ r =
MBS
( "HealthKit.isHealthDataAvailable" ); text = Case ( r = 1 ; "yes"; r = 0 ; "no" ; r )]; text)]

    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>text)</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>

functions.name: LDAP.AddJSON
functions.description: Adds an entry to a tree. The parent of the entry being added must already exist or the parent must be empty (equal to the root distinguished name) for an add operation to succeed. Sample JSON for structure: [{ "operation": "Add", "type": "xxx", "values": ["Hello", "World"] }, { "operation": "Add", "type": "yyy", "base64": true, "value": "SGVsbG8gV29ybGQ=" }, { "operation": "Add", "type": "zzz", "hex": true, "value": "48656C6C6F20576F726C64" }] Type is the name of the attribute, e.g. cn. Version 10.1 or newer accepts name instead of type for the key to match the search results. Version 10.3 adds new boolean fields "hex" and "base64". If true, we decode value from hex/base64 encoding and pass them as binary values.

parameters.name: LDAPRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the LDAP connection.

parameters.name: DN
parameters.description: The name of the entry to add.

parameters.name: JSON
parameters.description: The JSON to parse. Must be a JSON array with entries. Each entry has an element operation with Add, Replace, Delete or Increment. Also a node with name "type" and the type to set. Than you can pass with value a single value or with values a list of values.

examples.label: Example script to create new group:
examples.text_code:
# ========================================
# 	Purpose:
# 			Creates a new AD Group
# 	Returns:
#
$error
= Error code if unsuccessful
#
$error
= 0 for success
#
$resultText
= Text summary of the success or error
# 	Parameters:
#
$serverName
#
$serverDomain
#
$baseOU
#
$groupName
(base name only, excluding domain name)
#
$groupDomain
# 	Called from:
# 			(script) "Set AD Group (worker)"
# 	Author:
# 			John Munro (HJM) from Deutsche Schule Tokyo Yokohama
# 	Notes:
# 			none
# 	History:
# 			2021-05-26 HJM - created from DySIS-StudentAdmin version
# ========================================
#
Set Variable [ $! ; Value: #Assign ( Get ( ScriptParameter ) ) ]
#
# The branch in LDAP containing all active entries
Set Variable [
$searchBase
; Value: "dc=dsty,dc=ac,dc=jp" ]
#
Set Error Capture [ On ]
#
#  If debugging these parameters will be empty so fill with test data
If [
$serverName
= "" ]
Set Variable [
$serverName
; Value: "sn-sys-dc1" ]
End If
If [
$serverDomain
= "" ]
Set Variable [
$serverDomain
; Value: "schulnetz.dsty.ac.jp" ]
End If
If [
$baseOU
= "" ]
Set Variable [
$baseOU
; Value:
$searchBase
]
End If
If [
$groupName
= "" ]
Set Variable [
$groupName
; Value: "Test-Group" ]
End If
If [
$groupDomain
= "" ]
Set Variable [
$groupDomain
; Value: "dsty.ac.jp" ]
End If
#
#
#  Bind to LDAP
Perform Script [ Specified: From list ; “LDAPServerBind” ; Parameter: # ( "serverName" ;
$serverName
) & # ( "serverDomain" ;
$serverDomain
) ]
# Returns
$result
,
$ldap
#
Set Variable [ $! ; Value: #Assign ( Get ( ScriptResult ) ) ]
If [
$error
<> 0 ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "LDAP error" ;
$resultText
]
Exit Script [ Text Result: # ( "error" ;
$result
) & # ( "resultText" ; "LDAP error: " &
$resultText
) ]
End If
#
#
#  Sanity check: Ensure that group is not already present in AD
Set Variable [
$LDAPFilter
; Value: "(sAMAccountName=" &
$groupName
& ")" ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "
LDAP.Search
" ;
$ldap
;
$searchBase
; "subtree" ;
$LDAPFilter
; "" ; 0 ; 20 ; 9999 ) ]
#
Set Variable [
$entryCount
; Value:
MBS
("
LDAP.SearchResult.Count
";
$ldap
) ]
If [
$entryCount
> 0 ]
#  The group is already present in AD so exit with error
Set Variable [
$resultText
; Value: "The sAMAccountName is already present in AD: \" &
$groupName
" ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "LDAP Error" ;
$resultText
]
# Cleanup
Set Variable [
$releaseResult
; Value:
MBS
("
LDAP.Release
";
$ldap
) ]
Exit Script [ Text Result: # ( "error" ; 1 ) & # ( "resultText" ; "LDAP error: " &
$resultText
) ]
End If
#
#
# Sanity check: Ensure the DN is not already present in AD
Set Variable [
$groupDN
; Value: "CN=" &
$groupName
& "," &
$baseOU
]
Set Variable [
$LDAPFilter
; Value: "" ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "
LDAP.Search
" ;
$ldap
;
$groupDN
; "base" ;
$LDAPFilter
; "" ; 0 ; 4 ; 1 ) ]
#
Set Variable [
$entryCount
; Value:
MBS
("
LDAP.SearchResult.Count
";
$ldap
) ]
If [
$entryCount
> 0 ]
#  The groupname is already present in AD so exit with error
Set Variable [
$resultText
; Value: "The DN is already present in AD: " &
$groupDN
]
Show Custom Dialog [ "LDAP Error" ;
$resultText
]
# Cleanup
Set Variable [
$releaseResult
; Value:
MBS
("
LDAP.Release
";
$ldap
) ]
Exit Script [ Text Result: # ( "error" ; 1 ) & # ( "resultText" ; "LDAP error: " &
$resultText
) ]
End If
#
#
# ===============================================================================================
# Group is confirmed not present in AD so proceed to create it
# Build the JSON for the Add
#
# AD attributes
Set Variable [
$objectClass
; Value: "{   \"operation\": \"Add\",   \"name\": \"objectClass\",   \"values\": [ \"top\", \"group\" ]   }" ]
Set Variable [
$sAMAccountName
; Value: "{   \"operation\": \"Add\",   \"name\": \"sAMAccountName\",   \"value\": \"" &
$groupName
& "\" }" ]
Set Variable [
$cn
; Value: "{   \"operation\":   \"Add\",   \"name\":   \"cn\",   \"value\":   \"" &
$groupName
& "\" }" ]
Set Variable [
$mail
; Value: "{     \"operation\":   \"Add\",     \"name\":   \"mail\",     \"value\":   \"" & Lower (
$groupName
& "@" &
$groupDomain
) & "\"   }" ]
#
# Add the JSON components together
Set Variable [
$json
; Value: "[" &
$objectClass
& "," &
$sAMAccountName
& "," &
$cn
& "," &
$mail
& "]" ]
#
# Attempt to add the groupDN record to the
$baseOU
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "LDAP.AddJSON" ;
$ldap
;
$groupDN
;
$json
) ]
If [
MBS
( "IsError" ) ]
Set Variable [
$resultText
; Value: "Failed to add group." & ¶ &
$result
& ¶ &
$json
]
Show Custom Dialog [ "LDAP Error" ;
$resultText
]
# Cleanup
Set Variable [
$releaseResult
; Value:
MBS
("
LDAP.Release
";
$ldap
) ]
Exit Script [ Text Result: # ( "error" ;
$result
) & # ( "resultText" ; "LDAP error: " &
$resultText
) ]
End If
#
#
# Cleanup
Set Variable [
$releaseResult
; Value:
MBS
("
LDAP.Release
";
$ldap
) ]
Go to Layout [ original layout ; Animation: None ]
#
#
# Return error free result
Exit Script [ Text Result: # ( "error" ; 0 ) & # ( "resultText" ; "Group create success" ) ]

    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$baseOU</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $groupName = "" </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"Test-Group" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$groupName</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $groupDomain = "" </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"dsty.ac.jp" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$groupDomain</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>  Bind to LDAP</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="1" name="Perform Script">
    <Calculation># ( "serverName" ; $serverName ) & # ( "serverDomain" ; $serverDomain )</Calculation>
    <Script id="99999" name="LDAPServerBind"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Returns $result, $ldap</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>#Assign ( Get ( ScriptResult ) ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$!</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $error <> 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "LDAP error" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$resultText </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result: # ( "error" ; $result ) & # ( "resultText" ; "LDAP error: " & $resultText ) </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>  Sanity check: Ensure that group is not already present in AD</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"(sAMAccountName=" & $groupName & ")" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$LDAPFilter</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS ( "LDAP.Search" ; $ldap ; $searchBase ; "subtree" ; $LDAPFilter ; "" ; 0 ; 20 ; 9999 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("LDAP.SearchResult.Count"; $ldap) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$entryCount</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $entryCount > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>  The group is already present in AD so exit with error</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"The sAMAccountName is already present in AD: \" & $groupName" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$resultText</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "LDAP Error" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$resultText </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("LDAP.Release"; $ldap) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$releaseResult</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result: # ( "error" ; 1 ) & # ( "resultText" ; "LDAP error: " & $resultText ) </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Sanity check: Ensure the DN is not already present in AD</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"CN=" & $groupName & "," & $baseOU </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$groupDN</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$LDAPFilter</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS ( "LDAP.Search" ; $ldap ; $groupDN ; "base" ; $LDAPFilter ; "" ; 0 ; 4 ; 1 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("LDAP.SearchResult.Count"; $ldap) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$entryCount</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $entryCount > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>  The groupname is already present in AD so exit with error</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"The DN is already present in AD: " & $groupDN </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$resultText</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "LDAP Error" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$resultText </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("LDAP.Release"; $ldap) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$releaseResult</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result: # ( "error" ; 1 ) & # ( "resultText" ; "LDAP error: " & $resultText ) </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> ===============================================================================================</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Group is confirmed not present in AD so proceed to create it</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Build the JSON for the Add</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> AD attributes</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"{   \"operation\": \"Add\",   \"name\": \"objectClass\",   \"values\": [ \"top\", \"group\" ]   }" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$objectClass</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"{   \"operation\": \"Add\",   \"name\": \"sAMAccountName\",   \"value\": \"" & $groupName & "\" }" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$sAMAccountName</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"{   \"operation\":   \"Add\",   \"name\":   \"cn\",   \"value\":   \"" & $groupName & "\" }" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$cn</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"{     \"operation\":   \"Add\",     \"name\":   \"mail\",     \"value\":   \"" & Lower ( $groupName & "@" & $groupDomain ) & "\"   }" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$mail</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Add the JSON components together</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"[" &  $objectClass & "," &  $sAMAccountName & "," &  $cn & "," &  $mail & "]" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$json</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Attempt to add the groupDN record to the $baseOU</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "LDAP.AddJSON" ; $ldap ; $groupDN ; $json ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS( "IsError" ) </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"Failed to add group." & ¶ & $result & ¶ & $json </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$resultText</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "LDAP Error" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$resultText </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("LDAP.Release"; $ldap) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$releaseResult</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result: # ( "error" ; $result ) & # ( "resultText" ; "LDAP error: " & $resultText ) </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("LDAP.Release"; $ldap) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$releaseResult</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="6" name="Go to Layout">
    <LayoutDestination value="OriginalLayout"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Return error free result</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result: # ( "error" ; 0 ) & # ( "resultText" ; "Group create success" ) </Calculation>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Example script to create an user:
examples.text_code:
# ========================================
# 	Purpose:
# 			If the user is not present in AD, create a new user in Pre-handover OU and add the groups defined in DySIS
# 	Returns:
# 			0 for success
# 			Errot text if unsuccessful
# 	Parameters:
#
$serverName
#
$userName
#
$userDomain
#
$userEmail
#
$fullName
#
$surname
#
$givenName
#
$userOU
#
$groups
(base names only, excluding domain names)
#
$userPassword
# 	Called from:
# 			(script) Create AD User account
# 	Author:
# 			John Munro (HJM) from Deutsche Schule Tokyo Yokohama
# 	Notes:
# 			none
# 	History:
# 			2020-06-30 HJM - created
# 			2020-09-10 HJM - modified to add parameter
$userOU
# 			2020-12-18 HJM - modified PasswordSet to use external subroutine rather than local code
# 			2021-05-20 HJM - Replaced bind code with call to LDAPServerBind (including added parameter
$serverDomain
to all calls)
# ========================================
#
Set Variable [ $! ; Value: #Assign ( Get ( ScriptParameter ) ) ]
#
# ===============================================================================================
#  If debugging these parameters will be empty so fill with test data
If [
$serverName
= "" ]
Set Variable [
$serverName
; Value: "sys-dc1" ]
End If
If [
$serverName
= "" ]
Set Variable [
$serverDomain
; Value: "dsty.ac.jp" ]
End If
If [
$userName
= "" ]
Set Variable [
$userName
; Value: "DySIStestUser" ]
End If
If [
$userDomain
= "" ]
Set Variable [
$userDomain
; Value: "dsty.ac.jp" ]
End If
If [
$userEmail
= "" ]
Set Variable [
$userEmail
; Value: "dysistestUser@dsty.test" ]
End If
If [
$userPassword
= "" ]
Set Variable [
$userPassword
; Value: "Welcome2" ]
End If
If [
$fullName
= "" ]
Set Variable [
$fullName
; Value: "DySIS testUser" ]
End If
If [
$surname
= "" ]
Set Variable [
$surname
; Value: "testUser" ]
End If
If [
$givenName
= "" ]
Set Variable [
$givenName
; Value: "DySIStest" ]
End If
If [
$userOU
= "" ]
Set Variable [
$userOU
; Value: "OU=Pre-handover,OU=DSTY Groups,DC=dsty,DC=ac,DC=jp" ]
End If
# Note
$groups
as an empty set is a valid condition so this should NOT be filled if empty
#
#
#  Bind to LDAP
Perform Script [ Specified: From list ; “LDAPServerBind” ; Parameter: # ( "serverName" ;
$serverName
) & # ( "serverDomain" ;
$serverDomain
) ]
# Returns
$error
,
$resultText
,
$ldap
#
Set Variable [ $! ; Value: #Assign ( Get ( ScriptResult ) ) ]
If [
$error
<> 0 ]
Go to Layout [ original layout ; Animation: None ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "LDAP error" ;
$resultText
]
Exit Script [ Text Result: # ( "error" ;
$error
) & # ( "resultText" ; "LDAP error: " &
$resultText
) ]
End If
#
#
#  Sanity check: Ensure that user is not already present in AD
#
Set Variable [
$LDAPFilter
; Value: "(sAMAccountName=" &
$userName
& ")" ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "
LDAP.Search
" ;
$ldap
;
$searchBase
; "subtree" ;
$LDAPFilter
; "" ; 0 ; 20 ; 9999 ) ]
#
Set Variable [
$entryCount
; Value:
MBS
("
LDAP.SearchResult.Count
";
$ldap
) ]
If [
$entryCount
> 0 ]
#  The username is already present in AD so exit with error
Set Variable [
$errorText
; Value: "The sAMAccountName is already present in AD: \" &
$userName
" ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "LDAP Error" ;
$errorText
]
# Cleanup
Set Variable [
$releaseResult
; Value:
MBS
("
LDAP.Release
";
$ldap
) ]
Exit Script [ Text Result: "LDAP error. " &
$errorText
]
End If
#
# Sanity check: Ensure the DN is not already present in AD
#
Set Variable [
$personDN
; Value: "CN=" &
$fullName
& "," &
$userOU
]
Set Variable [
$LDAPFilter
; Value: "" ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "
LDAP.Search
" ;
$ldap
;
$personDN
; "base" ;
$LDAPFilter
; "" ; 0 ; 4 ; 1 ) ]
#
Set Variable [
$entryCount
; Value:
MBS
("
LDAP.SearchResult.Count
";
$ldap
) ]
If [
$entryCount
> 0 ]
#  The username is already present in AD so exit with error
Set Variable [
$errorText
; Value: "The DN is already present in AD: " &
$personDN
]
Show Custom Dialog [ "LDAP Error" ;
$errorText
]
# Cleanup
Set Variable [
$releaseResult
; Value:
MBS
("
LDAP.Release
";
$ldap
) ]
Exit Script [ Text Result: "LDAP error. " &
$errorText
]
End If
#
#
# ===============================================================================================
# User is confirmed not present in AD so proceed to create it
# Build the JSON for the Add
#
# AD attributes
Set Variable [
$objectClass
; Value: "{   \"operation\": \"Add\",   \"name\": \"objectClass\",   \"values\": [ \"top\", \"person\", \"organizationalPerson\", \"user\" ]   }" ]
Set Variable [
$sAMAccountName
; Value: "{   \"operation\": \"Add\",   \"name\": \"sAMAccountName\",   \"value\": \"" &
$userName
& "\" }" ]
Set Variable [
$userPrincipalName
; Value: "{   \"operation\": \"Add\",   \"name\": \"userPrincipalName\",   \"value\": \"" &
$userName
& "@" &
$userDomain
& "\" }" ]
Set Variable [
$userAccountControl
; Value: "{   \"operation\": \"Add\",   \"name\": \"userAccountControl\",   \"value\": \"" & 544 & "\" }" //NB: 544 is [ NoPasswordRequired, NormalAccount ] ]
Set Variable [
$cn
; Value: "{   \"operation\":   \"Add\",   \"name\":   \"cn\",   \"value\":   \"" &
$fullName
& "\" }" ]
Set Variable [
$displayName
; Value: "{     \"operation\":   \"Add\",     \"name\":   \"displayName\",     \"value\":   \"" &
$fullName
& "\"   }" ]
Set Variable [
$sn
; Value: "{     \"operation\":   \"Add\",     \"name\":   \"sn\",     \"value\":   \"" &
$surname
& "\"   }" ]
Set Variable [
$givenName
; Value: "{     \"operation\":   \"Add\",     \"name\":   \"givenName\",     \"value\":   \"" &
$givenName
& "\"   }" ]
Set Variable [
$mail
; Value: "{     \"operation\":   \"Add\",     \"name\":   \"mail\",     \"value\":   \"" &
$userEmail
& "\"   }" ]
#
# Add the JSON components together
Set Variable [
$json
; Value: "[" &
$objectClass
& "," &
$sAMAccountName
& "," &
$userPrincipalName
& "," &
$userAccountControl
& "," &
$cn
& "," &
$displayName
& "," &
$sn
& "," &
$givenName
& "," &
$mail
& "]" ]
#
# Attempt to add the personDN record to the default OU
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "LDAP.AddJSON" ;
$ldap
;
$personDN
;
$json
) ]
If [
MBS
( "IsError" ) ]
Set Variable [
$errorText
; Value: "Failed to add user." & ¶ &
$result
& ¶ &
$json
]
Show Custom Dialog [ "LDAP Error" ;
$errorText
]
# Cleanup
Set Variable [
$releaseResult
; Value:
MBS
("
LDAP.Release
";
$ldap
) ]
Exit Script [ Text Result: "LDAP error. " &
$errorText
]
End If
#
# ===============================================================================================
# Set the password
#
Perform Script [ Specified: From list ; “Set AD Password (worker)” ; Parameter: # ( "serverName" ;
$serverName
) & # ( "serverDomain" ;
$serverDomain
) & # ( "personDN" ;
$personDN
) & # ( "userPassword" ;
$userPassword
) ]
#
Set Variable [
$result
; Value: Get ( ScriptResult ) ]
If [
$result
<> 0 ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Error setting the password" ;
$result
]
# Exit with failure
Exit Script [ Text Result: "Error setting the password:¶" &
$result
]
End If
#
#
# ===============================================================================================
# Set the userAccountControl to NormalAccount (512)
# Prior to setting the password, the account has a password not required attribute
#
# Build the JSON for the modify
Set Variable [
$json
; Value: "[{   \"operation\": \"Replace\",   \"name\": \"userAccountControl\",   \"value\": \"512\" }]" ]
#
# Attempt to modify the groupDN record
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "
LDAP.ModifyJSON
" ;
$ldap
;
$personDN
;
$json
) ]
If [
MBS
( "IsError" ) ]
Set Variable [
$errorText
; Value: "Failed to set account to 'Normal account (type 512)'." & ¶ &
$result
& ¶ &
$json
]
Show Custom Dialog [ "LDAP Error" ;
$errorText
]
# Cleanup
Set Variable [
$releaseResult
; Value:
MBS
("
LDAP.Release
";
$ldap
) ]
Exit Script [ Text Result: "LDAP error. " &
$errorText
]
End If
#
#
# Cleanup
Set Variable [
$releaseResult
; Value:
MBS
("
LDAP.Release
";
$ldap
) ]
#
#
# ===============================================================================================
# Add the person to all the groups passed in
$groups
#
Set Variable [
$groupDomain
; Value:
$userDomain
]
#
Set Variable [
$groupIndex
; Value: 1 ]
Set Variable [
$groupCount
; Value: ValueCount (
$groups
) ]
If [
$groupCount
> 0 ]
#
Loop
Set Variable [
$groupName
; Value: GetValue (
$groups
;
$groupIndex
) ]
#
If [
$groupName
<> "" ]
#
# This worker script is already running on the server so do not nest it to a sub server script as this does not make sense nor work.
Perform Script [ Specified: From list ; “Set AD Group (worker)” ; Parameter: # ( "serverName" ;
$serverName
) & # ( "serverDomain" ;
$serverDomain
) & # ( "operation" ; "Add" ) & # ( "personDN" ;
$personDN
) & # ( "groupName" ;
$groupName
) & # ( "groupDomain" ;
$groupDomain
) ]
#
Set Variable [
$result
; Value: Get ( ScriptResult ) ]
If [
$result
<> 0 ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Add group error" ;
$result
]
Exit Script [ Text Result:
$result
]
End If
End If
#
Set Variable [
$groupIndex
; Value:
$groupIndex
+ 1 ]
Exit Loop If [
$groupIndex
>
$groupCount
]
#
End Loop
End If
#
# Exit with a success result
Exit Script [ Text Result: 0 ]

  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> 	Purpose:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> 			If the user is not present in AD, create a new user in Pre-handover OU and add the groups defined in DySIS</Text>
  </Step>

  </Step>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  </Step>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>  Bind to LDAP</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="1" name="Perform Script">
    <Calculation># ( "serverName" ; $serverName ) & # ( "serverDomain" ; $serverDomain )</Calculation>
    <Script id="99999" name="LDAPServerBind"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

    </Repetition>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS ( "LDAP.Search" ; $ldap ; $searchBase ; "subtree" ; $LDAPFilter ; "" ; 0 ; 20 ; 9999 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>

    </Repetition>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">

  </Step>

      <Calculation>"CN=" & $fullName & "," & $userOU </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$personDN</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$LDAPFilter</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS ( "LDAP.Search" ; $ldap ; $personDN ; "base" ; $LDAPFilter ; "" ; 0 ; 4 ; 1 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("LDAP.SearchResult.Count"; $ldap) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$entryCount</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $entryCount > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>  The username is already present in AD so exit with error</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"The DN is already present in AD: " & $personDN </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$errorText</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "LDAP Error" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>

    </Message>

      </Button>

      </Button>

  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("LDAP.Release"; $ldap) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$releaseResult</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> ===============================================================================================</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> User is confirmed not present in AD so proceed to create it</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Build the JSON for the Add</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> AD attributes</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"{   \"operation\": \"Add\",   \"name\": \"objectClass\",   \"values\": [ \"top\", \"person\", \"organizationalPerson\", \"user\" ]   }" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$objectClass</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"{   \"operation\": \"Add\",   \"name\": \"sAMAccountName\",   \"value\": \"" & $userName & "\" }" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$sAMAccountName</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"{   \"operation\": \"Add\",   \"name\": \"userPrincipalName\",   \"value\": \"" & $userName & "@" & $userDomain & "\" }" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$userPrincipalName</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"{   \"operation\": \"Add\",   \"name\": \"userAccountControl\",   \"value\": \"" & 544 & "\" }" //NB: 544 is [ NoPasswordRequired, NormalAccount ] </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$userAccountControl</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"{   \"operation\":   \"Add\",   \"name\":   \"cn\",   \"value\":   \"" & $fullName & "\" }" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$cn</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"{     \"operation\":   \"Add\",     \"name\":   \"displayName\",     \"value\":   \"" & $fullName & "\"   }" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$displayName</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"{     \"operation\":   \"Add\",     \"name\":   \"sn\",     \"value\":   \"" & $surname & "\"   }" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$sn</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"{     \"operation\":   \"Add\",     \"name\":   \"givenName\",     \"value\":   \"" & $givenName & "\"   }" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$givenName</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"{     \"operation\":   \"Add\",     \"name\":   \"mail\",     \"value\":   \"" & $userEmail & "\"   }" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$mail</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Add the JSON components together</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"[" &  $objectClass & "," &  $sAMAccountName & "," &  $userPrincipalName & "," &  $userAccountControl & "," &  $cn & "," &  $displayName & "," &  $sn & "," &  $givenName & "," &  $mail & "]" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$json</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Attempt to add the personDN record to the default OU</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "LDAP.AddJSON" ; $ldap ; $personDN ; $json ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS( "IsError" ) </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"Failed to add user." & ¶ & $result & ¶ & $json </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$errorText</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "LDAP Error" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$errorText </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>

  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>

  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Cleanup</Text>

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

functions.name: LDAP.Bind
functions.signature: MBS( "LDAP.Bind"; LDAPRef; DN; Cred; AuthMethod { ; Domain } )
functions.description: Authenticates a client to the LDAP server. See also SystemInfo.ADSystemInfo function to query details about logged in computer user.

parameters.name: LDAPRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the LDAP connection.

parameters.name: DN
parameters.description: Test that contains the distinguished name of the entry used to bind. This can be a DN, a UPN, a WinNT style user name, or other name that the directory server will accept as an identifier.

parameters.name: Cred
parameters.description: The credentials with which to authenticate. Arbitrary credentials can be passed using this parameter. The format and content of the credentials depends on the setting of the method parameter.

parameters.name: AuthMethod
parameters.description: The authentication method to use. Can be simple, SASL or Negotiate. On macOS, also none, KRBv4, KRBv41 and KRBv42 are accepted. On Windows, also Sicily, MSN, NTLM, DPA, SSPI, Digest and External are accepted.

parameters.name: Domain
parameters.description: Domain for Windows.

examples.label: Authenticate connection:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("LDAP.Bind"; $ldap; $UserName; $Password; $AuthMethod)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Example script to connect and bind:
examples.text_code:
# ========================================
# 	Purpose:
# 			Common routine to bind to the LDAP server
# 	Returns:
#
$error
= Error code if unsuccessful
#
$error
= 0 for success
#
$resultText
= Text summary of the success or error
# 	Parameters:
#
$serverName
#
$serverDomain
# 	Called from:
# 			(script) All "worker" LDAP scripts
# 	Author:
# 			John Munro (HJM) from Deutsche Schule Tokyo Yokohama
# 	Notes:
# 			none
# 	History:
# 			2021-05-20 HJM - created
# ========================================
#
Set Variable [ $! ; Value: #Assign ( Get ( ScriptParameter ) ) ]
Set Variable [
$bindUsername
; Value: "filemakerbind" ]
Set Variable [
$bindPassword
; Value: “xxxxxxxxx” ]
#
Set Error Capture [ On ]
#
#  If debugging these parameters will be empty so fill with test data
If [
$serverName
= "" ]
Set Variable [
$serverName
; Value: "sys-xxx” ]
End If
If [
$serverDomain
= "" ]
Set Variable [
$serverDomain
; Value: “xxx.com” ]
End If
#
Set Variable [
$serverFQDN
; Value:
$serverName
& "." &
$serverDomain
]
#
#  Connect
Set Variable [
$ssl
; Value: 1 ]
Set Variable [
$port
; Value: 636 ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "
LDAP.Connect
" ;
$serverFQDN
;
$ssl
;
$port
) ]
If [
MBS
( "IsError" ) ]
// Show Custom Dialog [ "LDAP Error" ; "Failed to connect to Domain Controller." & ¶ &
$result
]
Exit Script [ Text Result: # ( "error" ;
$result
) & # ( "resultText" ; "LDAP error. Failed to connect to Domain Controller." & ¶ &
$result
) ]
End If
#
#  Login
Set Variable [
$ldap
; Value:
$result
]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("LDAP.Bind";
$ldap
;
$bindUsername
& "@" &
$serverDomain
;
$bindPassword
; "simple") ]
If [
MBS
( "IsError" ) ]
// Show Custom Dialog [ "LDAP Error" ; "Failed to authenticate." & ¶ &
$result
]
Exit Script [ Text Result: # ( "error" ;
$result
) & # ( "resultText" ; "LDAP error. Failed to authenticate." & ¶ &
$result
) ]
End If
#
# Return error free result
Exit Script [ Text Result: # ( "error" ; 0 ) & # ( "resultText" ; "Bind successful" ) & # ( "ldap" ;
$ldap
) ]

functions.name: LDAP.Connect
functions.description: Initializes an LDAP connection. Returns the references number which you pass to other LDAP functions. Please call LDAP.Release later to close connection. Before connecting, you can use EnvironmentVariables.SetValue function to set various flags for LDAP library, e.g. LDAPREFERRALS option. Please check LDAP documentation for which environment variables can be set.

parameters.name: IP
parameters.description: The IP or domain name of the server. If prefixed with ldap:// or ldaps://, the plugin will remove that prefix. If prefix is ldaps://, the plugin will assume SSL is enabled.

parameters.name: SSL
parameters.description: Whether to use SSL. 1 to enable or 0 to not enable. If you start with 0 here, you can later use LDAP.StartTLS to enable TLS.

parameters.name: Port
parameters.description: The port to use. If zero or undefined, the default port is used. e.g. 636 for LDAPS or 389 for LDAP.

examples.label: Connect to local LDAP server:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "LDAP.Connect"; "localhost"; 0; 389 )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "LDAP.Connect"; "localhost"; 0; 389 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Connect to LDAP server:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "LDAP.Connect"; "ldap.internal"; 1)
