functions.name: OCR.GetVariable
functions.description: Queries a variable.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the variable.

functions.name: OCR.Initialize
functions.description: Initializes tesseract. Pass path to parent folder of tessdata folder and language you need. The language is (usually) an ISO 639-3 string. To use multiple languages, please concat them with plus sign: e.g. "eng+deu" You find our language files archive here: https://www.monkeybreadsoftware.com/filemaker/files/Libs/ The tessdata folder may need to contain additional files for some languages. If those are missing, the tesseract engine likes to abort the process. Starting with version 11.2 of MBS Plugin, the initialization is per thread. Scripts running on FileMaker server may need to check OCR.IsInitialized and do initialization for their execution thread if needed before using OCR functions. But then each script can do OCR in parallel using multiple CPU cores. To use tesseract 4.x, please use OCR.Load function to load the newer libraries. Please notice that using data files for version 3 with library of version 4 or 5 (or other way around) will crash. We intent to removed tesseract 3.x library from the plugin in version 15.5, so please move to loading the newer version. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The path to the folder where to find tessdata folder. Must end with tessdata as folder name. Can be empty if tesseract libraries know where their files are.

parameters.name: Lang
parameters.description: The language to use.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: For tessertact 4 or newer, pass here optional option list.

examples.label: Initialize with tessdata folder in same folder as database for english:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$databasePath
; Value: Get(FilePath) ]
Set Variable [
$databasePath
; Value:
MBS
("
Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath
";
$databasePath
) ]
Set Variable [
$databasePath
; Value: Substitute (
$databasePath
; ".fp7" ; ".fmp12") ]
Set Variable [
$databaseName
; Value: Get(FileName) & ".fmp12" ]
Set Variable [
$path
; Value: Substitute (
$databasePath
;
$databaseName
; "tessdata" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "OCR.Initialize";
$path
; "eng") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Get(FilePath) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$databasePath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Path.FilemakerPathToNativePath"; $databasePath) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$databasePath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Substitute ( $databasePath ; ".fp7" ; ".fmp12") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$databasePath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Get(FileName) & ".fmp12" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$databaseName</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Substitute ( $databasePath ; $databaseName ; "tessdata" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "OCR.Initialize"; $path; "eng") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: OCR.IsInitialized
functions.description: Checks if OCR library has been initialized. If value is 1, everything is okay. If value is zero, the library is not initialized and you need to call OCR.Initialize function. On a hosted database this function is better than using a global variable, as the variable can't see if initialization was made on server or client side. Starting with version 11.2 of MBS Plugin, the initialization is per thread. Scripts running on FileMaker server may need to check OCR.IsInitialized and do initialization for their execution thread if needed before using OCR functions. But then each script can do OCR in parallel using multiple CPU cores.

examples.label: Initialize on demand:
examples.text_code:
If [
MBS
("OCR.IsInitialized") ≠ 1]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
OCR.Initialize
"; "/Library/FileMaker Server/"; "eng")]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [MBS("OCR.IsInitialized") ≠ 1]
	Set Variable [$r; Value:MBS( "OCR.Initialize"; "/Library/FileMaker Server/"; "eng")]
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: OCR.Load
functions.description: Loads newer tesseract engine. You can load here a version of tesseract 4.0 or newer. You may first need to load leptonica library via this function and then the tesseract library, if it is not in the same library file. For Linux you can install tesseract like this for library and whatever language file you need: sudo apt-get install libtesseract5 tesseract-ocr-deu For macOS you can use homebrew package manager or compile and install your own version. https://formulae.brew.sh/formula/tesseract For Windows, you may find a recent builds here: https://digi.bib.uni-mannheim.de/tesseract/?C=M;O=D On Windows you may need to call Process.SetDllDirectory function to tell Windows where to find dependencies for the python DLL.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native path to the library.

examples.label: Load it on Linux:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "OCR.Load"; "liblept.so
.5
" ) &
MBS
( "OCR.Load"; "libtesseract.so
.5
" )
// please install tesseract library, so plugin can load it:
// sudo apt-get install libtesseract5
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "OCR.Load"; "liblept.so.5" ) & 
MBS( "OCR.Load"; "libtesseract.so.5" )

// please install tesseract library, so plugin can load it:
// sudo apt-get install libtesseract5</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: OCR.SetImage
functions.description: Provide an image for Tesseract to recognize. The plugin makes a copy of the image. Please do not pass PDF files. For PDF you either need to extract image (for scans) or raster PDF pages as images. Our functions PDFKit.GetPDFPageImage , WinPDF.PageImage or DynaPDF.RenderPage can help here. If the PDF file contains real text, please use PDFKit.GetPDFText , PDFKit.GetPDFPageText , DynaPDF.ExtractText , DynaPDF.ExtractPageText or DynaPDF.ExtractPageRectText . This will not do OCR, but extract the text directly. Optionally you can define area of interest with OCR.SetRectangle function.

parameters.name: ImageRef
parameters.description: The image reference. Use functions like GMImage.NewFromContainer to get an image reference.

examples.label: Set image reference:
examples.xml_code:

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

Be aware that if the font loaded in pdf page is wider than scanned font, the words can overlap. Plugin version 7.5 or newer will reduce font size if needed to fit word length to fix this.

MBS( "OCR.WriteToPDF"; $x; $y; $w; $h)</Calculation>

functions.name: ServiceProvider.Install
functions.description: Installs a service provider handler. If you add right info.plist entries to the info.plist inside the FileMaker app, you register services. The plugin starts a script when a service request is received. Best call this function in the script called when solutions opens. This function has been verified to work well for file and text services. Other types are also implemented, but not tested. see also: https://developer.apple.com/library/mac/documentation/Cocoa/Conceptual/SysServices/introduction.html With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the FileMaker File that contains the script.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to run.

parameters.name: Force
parameters.description: Asks system to update service menu now.

functions.name: ServiceProvider.Uninstall
functions.description: Removes service provider. This call is not required. Cleanup is done automatically when app exits.

functions.name: ServiceProvider.Value
functions.description: Queries a value from last service invocation. If you got a file, you can query file with Paths to get a list of all file paths as a list. For URLs, you get a list of all URLs when you pass "URLs".

parameters.name: Selector
parameters.description: Which value to query. Can be URL, Text, RTF, HTML, Paths, PDF, Image, URL or Path.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: Optionally, the file name to use for the container value when returning image or PDF.

parameters.name: DontRenderPreview
parameters.description: Pass 1 to not create a JPEG with preview of first page as part of the container.

functions.name: Trace
functions.description: Starts logging all plugin calls to a text file. If you pass a path, debug messages are written to the file. Else the plugin writes to debug console. See Console.app on Mac or DebugView on Windows. See also Trace.Off and Trace.SetErrorsOnly . On Linux, if no path is provided, the log output will go to stderr, which is usually redirected by our plugin to /FileMaker/FileMaker Server/Logs/ folder. The log files are StdErrWeb.log and StdOutWeb.log for Web Direct and StdErrServerScripting.log and StdOutServerScripting.log for normal scripting (the names may change in different plugin versions). On Windows with a FileMaker Server, you can see debug messages in DebugView if you run DebugView application as admin and enable global Win32 listening. For FileMaker Server on macOS you can see messages in /Library/FileMaker Server/Logs/stderr file. On Windows the log file is UTF-16 with BOM and CRLF as line endings. On macOS, Linux and iOS, you get an UTF-8 file with BOM and LF line endings. See Trace.GetErrorsOnly , Trace.GetWithTimes , Trace.SetErrorsOnly and Trace.SetWithTimes to set options. Added tracing of SQL commands for our SQL functions for v13.1. The SQL may be truncated around 4000 characters to limit how much text we log. Log files paths for MBS Plugin on Linux:

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native file path to the log file.

parameters.name: Append
parameters.description: Whether to append or create new file. Pass 1 to append or 0 (default) for new file.

( "Trace" )

</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Trace.AreErrorNotificationsEnabled
functions.description: Checks whether error notifications are enabled. See also Trace.DisableErrorNotifications or Trace.EnableErrorNotifications .

functions.name: Trace.DisableErrorNotifications
functions.description: Disables error notifications. See also Trace.AreErrorNotificationsEnabled or Trace.EnableErrorNotifications .

functions.name: Trace.EnableErrorNotifications
functions.description: Enable error notifications. The plugin presents errors returned by plugin calls as notifications, so you more easier notice them. Notifications are only presented at most one per 30 seconds. They are not shown if last error is same as new error or if you run plugin on server or runtime. The plugin will close error notifications after 5 seconds. See also Trace.DisableErrorNotifications or Trace.AreErrorNotificationsEnabled .

functions.name: Trace.GetErrorsOnly
functions.description: Queries whether to trace only errors.

functions.name: Trace.GetServerLogPath
functions.description: Queries server log path.

functions.name: Trace.GetWithTimes
functions.description: Queries whether to trace with times.

functions.name: Trace.IsEnabled
functions.description: Queries trace on/off status. Returns 1 if trace has been enabled or 0 if not. Logging happens only if trace is enabled and not paused.

functions.name: Trace.IsPaused
functions.description: Queries pause state of trace function. Returns 1 if paused or 0 if not. Logging happens only if trace is enabled and not paused.

functions.name: Trace.Off
functions.description: Disables tracing.

functions.name: Trace.Path
functions.description: Queries current trace path. Only when Trace function is called before with a file path.

; Value:

functions.name: Trace.Pause
functions.description: Pauses trace logging until you resume. For every call to Trace.Pause, we need a call to Trace.Resume .

functions.name: Trace.Resume
functions.description: Resumes trace logging. For every call to Trace.Pause , we need a call to Trace.Resume.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting.

functions.name: Trace.SetServerLogPath
functions.description: Enables logging per thread for server. Only useful on server, where multiple scripts may run in parallel and each may log to its own file. We suggest to use /tmp folder as this gets cleaned on server restart, but FileMaker's log folder may also work. Does nothing if called on client. Be aware that FileMaker Server reuses threads and the logs append the files, so when a script is done, the log file gets reused for another script. See also Trace.SetWithTimes to enable times in trace log. Since this function also enables trace if not yet running, there is no need to call Trace function. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native folder path.

examples.label: Set tmp as log folder:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Trace.SetServerLogPath"; "/tmp" ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Trace.SetServerLogPath"; "/tmp" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Enable advanced logging to debug a problem with CURL:

$r
; Value:

( "Trace"; "/tmp/
mbs
.log" ) ]

$r
; Value:
MBS
( "Trace.SetWithTimes"; 1 ) ]
#

Set Variable [
$r
; Value:

( "
CURL.SetDebugWithTime
";
$curl
; 1 ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
CURL.SetDebugWithProgress
";
$curl
; 1 ) ]

$r
; Value:

( "
CURL.SetOptionConnectTimeoutMS
";
$curl
; 10000 ) ]

functions.name: Trace.WriteLine

parameters.name: Parameters...
parameters.description: The parameters to print to the trace line. Can be several parameters in various data types.

("Trace.WriteLine"; "Hello: "; 123)

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

Export Field Contents [
Kontakte::Foto
; “
$path
” ; Create folders: Off ]

$r
; Value: Let ( [
e = Get(LastError);
r = If( e ≠ 0;
MBS
("Trace.WriteLine"; "Export all field contents: "; e); "")
]; r ) ]

    <CreateEmail state="False"/>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

functions.name: WinPDF.LoadContainer
functions.description: Loads a PDF document from data in container. Requires Windows 10 or newer. If the Portable Document Format (PDF) document does not require a password, this method ignores it. This method reports an ERROR_WRONG_PASSWORD (1323) error if the wrong password is specified.

parameters.name: ContainerValue
parameters.description: The container value (variable or field) to read the PDF from.

parameters.name: Password
parameters.description: The password to open the Portable Document Format (PDF) document, if it requires one.

examples.label: Make preview of page:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$pdf
; Value:
MBS
( "WinPDF.LoadContainer";
Get Preview::Input
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0 ]
Set Variable [
$NewImage
; Value:
MBS
( "
WinPDF.PageImageAtSize
";
$pdf
; 0; 0; 600; "JPEG"; "page.jpg") ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0 ]
Set Field [
Get Preview::Preview
;
$NewImage
]
Set Field [
Get Preview::Made
using ; "WinPDF" ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
WinPDF.Release
";
$pdf
) ]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
WinPDF.Release
";
$pdf
) ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WinPDF.LoadContainer"; Get Preview::Input) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pdf</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") = 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WinPDF.PageImageAtSize"; $pdf; 0; 0; 600; "JPEG"; "page.jpg") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$NewImage</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") = 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$NewImage </Calculation>
    <Field table="Get Preview" id="99999" name="Preview "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>"WinPDF" </Calculation>
    <Field table="Get Preview" id="99999" name="Made using "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WinPDF.Release"; $pdf) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result:    </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WinPDF.Release"; $pdf) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WinPDF.LoadFile
functions.description: Loads a PDF document from file synchronously. Requires Windows 10 or newer. If the Portable Document Format (PDF) document does not require a password, this method ignores it. This method reports an ERROR_WRONG_PASSWORD (1323) error if the wrong password is specified. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: Native file path to the PDF file.

parameters.name: Password
parameters.description: The password to open the Portable Document Format (PDF) document, if it requires one.

examples.label: Load a PDF from disk:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$pdf
; Value:
MBS
( "WinPDF.LoadFile"; "C:\test\test.pdf" ) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to open PDF." ;
$pdf
]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WinPDF.LoadFile"; "C:\test\test.pdf" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pdf</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to open PDF." </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$pdf </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result:    </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

$page

$page

( "WinPDF.PageBox";
$pdf
;
$page
; "MediaBox") ]

MBS
( "WinPDF.PageBox";
$pdf
;
$page

functions.name: WinPDF.PageImage
functions.signature: MBS( "WinPDF.PageImage"; PDFRef; Page { ; ImageType; FileName; DPI } )
functions.description: Renders a page to an image. Outputs a stream of data, which represents a Portable Document Format (PDF) page's contents as image. Takes a set of display settings, applies them to the output of a Portable Document Format (PDF) page's contents, and creates a stream with the customized, rendered output as an synchronous action. This asynchronous action can be used to create a customized display image of the Portable Document Format (PDF) page. See also DynaPDF.RenderPage for cross platform projects or PDFKit.GetPDFPageImage for macOS and iOS.

parameters.name: PDFRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the PDF document.

parameters.name: Page
parameters.description: The page index. Zero based.

parameters.name: ImageType
parameters.description: The desired image type. JPEG, PNG, BMP, TIFF, GIF or HEIF. Default is JPEG.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name to use for the returned image.

parameters.name: DPI
parameters.description: The resolution you like to use.

examples.label: Render preview in one Let statement:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
// open from container value in variable
pdf = MBS( "WinPDF.LoadContainer"; $Container );
e1 = MBS("isError");
page = 0;
// render preview
image = If(e1; ""; MBS( "WinPDF.PageImage"; pdf; page; "JPEG"; "preview.jpg"; 144));
e2 = MBS("isError");
// free resources
r = MBS( "WinPDF.Release"; pdf)
]; 
// if no error, return image
If(e2; ""; image))</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WinPDF.PageImageAtSize
functions.signature: MBS( "WinPDF.PageImageAtSize"; PDFRef; Page; Width; Height { ; ImageType; FileName } )
functions.description: Renders a page to an image. Outputs a stream of data, which represents a Portable Document Format (PDF) page's contents as image. Takes a set of display settings, applies them to the output of a Portable Document Format (PDF) page's contents, and creates a stream with the customized, rendered output as an synchronous action. This asynchronous action can be used to create a customized display image of the Portable Document Format (PDF) page. The given width and height is a requested size for the PDF engine in Windows, which may to pick its own size.

parameters.name: PDFRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the PDF document.

parameters.name: Page
parameters.description: The page index. Zero based.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: The page width to use. If zero, we calculate it proportionally from the height.

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: The page height to use. If zero, we calculate it proportionally from the width.

parameters.name: ImageType
parameters.description: The desired image type. JPEG, PNG, BMP, TIFF, GIF or HEIF. Default is JPEG.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name to use for the returned image.

examples.label: Make preview of page:
examples.text_code:
If [
MBS
("IsWindows") ]
Set Variable [
$pdf
; Value:
MBS
( "
WinPDF.LoadContainer
";
Get Preview::Input
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0 ]
Set Variable [
$NewImage
; Value:
MBS
( "WinPDF.PageImageAtSize";
$pdf
; 0; 0; 600; "JPEG"; "page.jpg") ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0 ]
Set Field [
Get Preview::Preview
;
$NewImage
]
Set Field [
Get Preview::Made
using ; "WinPDF" ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
WinPDF.Release
";
$pdf
) ]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
WinPDF.Release
";
$pdf
) ]
End If
Else If [
MBS
("IsMacOS") or
MBS
("IsIOS") ]
Set Variable [
$preview
; Value:
MBS
( "
PDFKit.GetPDFPageImage
";
$Input
; 0; "JPEG"; "input.jpg"; 72) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0 ]
Set Field [
Get Preview::Preview
;
$preview
]
Set Field [
Get Preview::Made
using ; "PDFKit" ]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [ MBS("IsWindows") ] 
	Set Variable [ $pdf ; Value: MBS( "WinPDF.LoadContainer"; Get Preview::Input) ] 
	If [ MBS("IsError") = 0 ] 
		Set Variable [ $NewImage ; Value: MBS( "WinPDF.PageImageAtSize"; $pdf; 0; 0; 600; "JPEG"; "page.jpg") ] 
		If [ MBS("IsError") = 0 ] 
			Set Field [ Get Preview::Preview ; $NewImage ] 
			Set Field [ Get Preview::Made using ; "WinPDF" ] 
			Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS( "WinPDF.Release"; $pdf) ] 
			Exit Script [ Text Result:    ] 
		End If
		Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS( "WinPDF.Release"; $pdf) ] 
	End If
Else If [ MBS("IsMacOS") or MBS("IsIOS") ] 
	Set Variable [ $preview ; Value: MBS( "PDFKit.GetPDFPageImage"; $Input; 0; "JPEG"; "input.jpg"; 72) ] 
	If [ MBS("IsError") = 0 ] 
		Set Field [ Get Preview::Preview ; $preview ] 
		Set Field [ Get Preview::Made using ; "PDFKit" ] 
		Exit Script [ Text Result:    ] 
	End If
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WinPDF.Release
functions.description: Releases an object reference. Please call that for all PDF objects to release memory.

parameters.name: PDFRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the PDF document.

examples.label: Query page count from a PDF:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$pdf
; Value:
MBS
( "
WinPDF.LoadContainer
";
WinPDF::InputPDF
;
WinPDF::Password
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ Title: "Failed to open PDF."; Message:
$pdf
; Default Button: “OK”, Commit: “Yes” ]
Exit Script [ ]
End If
# query page count
Set Variable [
$count
; Value:
MBS
( "
WinPDF.PageCount
";
$pdf
) ]
Set Field [
WinPDF::PageCount
;
$count
]
# cleanup
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "WinPDF.Release";
$pdf
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WinPDF.LoadContainer"; WinPDF::InputPDF; WinPDF::Password ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pdf</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> Title: "Failed to open PDF."; Message: $pdf</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>Default Button: “OK”, Commit: “Yes” </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> query page count</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WinPDF.PageCount"; $pdf) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$count </Calculation>
    <Field table="WinPDF" id="99999" name="PageCount"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WinPDF.Release"; $pdf) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>
